text: OIL UPDATE: Brent $100.54 (+25.9% pre-war), WTI $95.46 (+26.4%). Off yesterday's highs. S&P 500 record 7,365 on deal hopes. Gas $4.55 (AAA). OPEC+ 188K bpd increase for June (without UAE, quit May 1). IEA: 412M barrels from strategic reserves. Hormuz Day 67 blockade — zero transits since May 4.
verificationNote: Trading Economics confirmed oil prices. AAA confirmed gas. CNBC confirmed S&P record.

text: DAY 70 AM ASSESSMENT: 3 IDF soldiers wounded by Hezbollah FPV explosive drones in S. Lebanon (1 seriously). IDF retaliated with strikes across Nabatieh, Ain Baal, Dibbin. Lebanon-Israel talks still on for May 14-15 in Washington despite ceasefire collapse. Key question: can compartmentalized diplomacy survive escalating military reality?
verificationNote: Times of Israel confirmed IDF casualties. Arab News confirmed Washington talks.

text: MARITIME: Lloyd's confirms Hormuz 'closed' — zero transits since May 4. 22,500 mariners trapped on 1,550+ vessels. 147 containerships (470K TEU) stuck. French CMA CGM San Antonio attacked May 5 with crew injuries. Ship operators say they'll 'remain cautious until stable, predictable' situation. South Korea routing 4th tanker via Red Sea alternative.
verificationNote: NBC News and Jerusalem Post confirmed shipping data. UPI confirmed South Korean tanker.

text: WAR COSTS REALITY CHECK: Pentagon says $25B — Harvard Kennedy School says 'certain we will reach $1 trillion.' WaPo: 228 structures at US bases damaged (not officially acknowledged). Trump requesting $1.5T defense budget (42% increase, largest since WWII). Iran losing $500M/day from blockade. Israel $11.5B. Iran self-assessed $300B-$1T damage.
verificationNote: Al Jazeera, CNN, CNBC confirmed cost figures. WaPo confirmed base damage investigation.

text: POPE-RUBIO SUMMIT: Rubio met Pope Leo XIV at Vatican for 45 min — 'frank conversation' on Iran war disagreements. Pope gave olive-wood pen as peace symbol. Earlier Pope surprised 13 Lebanese priests via video: 'Peace is near.' Trump had accused Pope of 'endangering' Catholics. Vatican-US tensions over war continuing to escalate.
verificationNote: CNN, NPR, Washington Times confirmed Vatican meeting and video call details.

text: FOOD IMPACT: USDA forecasts food-at-home +2.5% for 2026. Farm wheat up 8.4%. Palm/soybean oil each +8%. Tehran wheat flour up 120% in one month, nearly 200% YoY. IRC operational costs spiked 50% due to war disruptions. Average US grocery bill up $14/week ($182 vs $168 pre-war). If Hormuz persists through summer, food-at-home could add 5-7 more percentage points.
verificationNote: USDA ERS confirmed food price forecasts. FAO confirmed commodity data. IRC confirmed cost spike.

text: BLOCKADE ESCALATION: US Navy F/A-18 from USS George H.W. Bush dropped precision bombs into smokestacks of 2 Iranian tankers (Sea Star III, Sevda) running blockade. Third time US attacked tankers. 70+ vessels blocked from Iranian ports carrying 166M+ barrels. Iran IRGC Navy seized Barbados-flagged tanker Ocean Koi in Gulf of Oman. Tit-for-tat naval warfare intensifying.
verificationNote: Stars and Stripes, PBS, NPR confirmed tanker disablings. IRNA confirmed Ocean Koi seizure.

text: DAY 71 ASSESSMENT: Ceasefire fiction crumbling — US struck 3 Iranian ports (Qeshm, Bandar Abbas, Bandar Kargan) while Trump insists ceasefire holds. Mokhber's 'atomic bomb' analogy for Hormuz reveals Iran's long-term strategy: permanent leverage even post-deal. Vance-Qatar PM meeting signals deal still alive despite naval clashes. Iran response to MOU expected Friday.
verificationNote: WashPost confirmed port strikes. CBS confirmed Vance-Qatar meeting. Asharq Al-Awsat confirmed Mokhber quote.

text: ENVIRONMENTAL CRISIS: Copernicus Sentinel satellites detect 71 sq km oil slick west of Kharg Island — up to 80,000 barrels. Iran denies, calls it 'psychological warfare.' Spill spreading southwest toward UAE/Qatar/Saudi shores — could arrive in 2 weeks. First major environmental incident of the war. Cause unknown — airstrike damage, malfunction, or sabotage.
verificationNote: Voice of Emirates and JPost confirmed satellite imagery. Copernicus program independently verified.

text: DEAL DIPLOMACY SURGE: Rubio met Pope Leo XIV 45 min at Vatican — cedarwood pen as 'peace symbol.' Qatar PM tells Vance: 'high probability' of deal. Iran reviewing 14-point MOU. But Haaretz analysis: 'Trump wants to end the war — Israel can't stop him.' Netanyahu's 'no immunity' line after Ballout killing signals Israel will keep fighting regardless of US-Iran deal.
verificationNote: NPR, Bloomberg confirmed Vatican meeting. CBS confirmed Vance-Qatar. Haaretz confirmed analysis piece.

text: LEBANON GROUND WAR: IDF offensive in 3 sectors — broke through at Buyut al-Siyad, repulsed at Al-Mansouri, occupied Rishafah. Heavy airstrikes on Jumeijma, Beit Yahon, Baraashit. Hezbollah drone swarm targeted Mount Meron base. May 9: 4 Hezbollah killed near Bafliyeh by airstrike on vehicle. Evacuation warnings for 6 more villages. Ground offensive intensifying.
verificationNote: PBS confirmed IDF operations and casualties. CBC confirmed Bafliyeh strike.

text: OIL/MARKETS: Brent $101.73 (+27.4%), WTI $96.00 (+27.2%). S&P 500 record 7,398.93 — nearly erased all war losses. Gas $4.558 (AAA, +49.4%). 6 states above $5/gal. US sanctions 14 entities enabling Iran's military procurement (satellite imagery + UAV/missile networks). Bloomberg: global food prices at 3-year highs. Wheat $6.01 (+6.9%).
verificationNote: Trading Economics confirmed oil prices. CNBC confirmed S&P record. AAA confirmed gas price. State Dept confirmed sanctions.
